notes.note: Sinuessa, a town on the Via Appia at the Latium-Campania border, where Cicero kept a modest staging-house (deversoriolum) for breaking the journey south; Cornificius had slighted it by passing it by.

notes.anchor: at the Cumanum and the Pompeianum
notes.note: Cicero's seaside villas near Cumae and at Pompeii, two of the several country properties on the Bay of Naples where he wrote and entertained; he jokingly demands that Cornificius leave a memorial of a visit there to placate the snubbed Sinuessan lodge.

notes.anchor: make her a record of your visit
notes.note: Cicero glosses the demand with the Greek hypomnematismon, "a memorandum" or written record, playfully treating the slighted little villa as a person owed a guest-book entry.

notes.anchor: This I scribbled while in the Senate.
notes.note: A throwaway sign-off: Cicero dashed off the note during a sitting of the Senate, an aside that conveys both haste and the informality of his friendship with Cornificius.
